meta.purpose: Define capability stages and measurable growth for TDD judgment.
meta.load_condition: Load when coaching, improvement, or practice measurement is requested.

stages.name: Test-After Coder
stages.description: Writes implementation first, then adds tests. Tests verify what the code does, not what it should do. May write horizontal test suites. Mocks liberally without considering behavioral boundaries.
indicators: Implements the feature, then writes tests to match
Test suite is organized by layer (unit, integration, E2E) rather than by behavior

stages.name: Vertical-Slice TDD Practitioner
stages.description: Follows RED-GREEN-REFACTOR for each vertical slice. Writes behavioral tests before implementation. One test at a time. Simplest passing implementation. Refactors only on GREEN. Mocks at boundaries only.
indicators: Writes failing test before implementation for each behavior
Organizes tests around behaviors, not layers
Hardcodes during GREEN when only one test case exists
Refactors only when all tests are GREEN
Mocks at system boundaries, not between internal components

stages.name: TDD Strategist
stages.description: Selects the right testing granularity for each situation. Knows when a unit test suffices vs. when an integration test is needed. Identifies and creates proper test seams. Designs testable architecture proactively.
indicators: Chooses test granularity based on where the behavior boundary is, not by rote
Identifies missing test seams and creates them through architecture changes
Uses tracer bullets to validate architecture before building out features
Balances test speed and coverage deliberately

evolution_layers.name: From test-after to vertical-slice TDD
evolution_layers.capability: Consistently write behavioral tests before implementation and organize work around vertical slices

evolution_layers.name: From practitioner to strategist
evolution_layers.capability: Design testable architecture and select appropriate testing strategies for each situation

measurement.how: Count the number of implementation tasks where a failing test was written before the implementation, divided by total implementation tasks.
Count tests that specify observable behavior (what) vs. tests that verify implementation details (how). Divide behavioral by total.
Count the number of test failures caused by implementation changes that preserved behavior, divided by total implementation changes.
